title: Why Vendor Portals Matter For Warranty Claims
subheader: Helmful helps dealers and vendors keep warranty claims, documents, feedback, approvals, and rejections tied to the same record.
featuredImagePrompt: A marine service manager reviewing warranty claim photos and documents on a laptop while a vendor representative joins on a tablet video call. Boat service bay background, professional style, no text.
body:
Warranty claims slow down when everything happens through email.

Photos get separated from the claim. A vendor asks for more detail. Someone approves part of the work, but the approval is buried in a thread. The customer waits while the dealership tries to confirm what happened.

What Helmful Does

Helmful includes a vendor portal for warranty claim review.

Approved vendor contacts can see assigned claims, review documents, provide line feedback, and approve or reject claims from one focused portal.

How It Helps

The dealership keeps the claim record organized. The vendor gets the information they need without asking for another forwarded email. Service managers can see claim status and follow up before a job sits too long.

This is especially helpful when claims include multiple parts, labor lines, photos, or supporting documents.

Why Access Control Matters

Vendor access should be limited. Helmful supports vendor portal access and verification so manufacturers and vendor contacts only see the warranty work assigned to them.

That keeps the process cleaner for the dealer, the vendor, and the customer waiting on an answer.
